title: Christ's Sadness.
content:
Christ was not sad, i' th' garden, for His own
    Passion, but for His sheep's dispersion.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Christ's Sadness.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
